title: Component Library
description: Complete reference for all 25 components available to the AI layout engine. Each entry defines the component type string, required/optional props, ideal use cases, and a valid JSON example. The layout engine MUST only use components defined here.

components.description: Full-viewport opening section with animated title, optional background image, and scroll-down indicator. Sets the tone for the entire course.
components.when_to_use: Always use as the FIRST component. Every course starts with a hero. Use the course title and a short subtitle that frames the learning journey.
components.when_not_to_use: Never use more than once per course.

required.displayTitle: string — Course title, large animated text
required.body: string (HTML) — Subtitle or course description, 1-2 sentences

_graphic.large: string — URL for background image (16:9, will be overlaid with dark scrim)
_graphic.alt: string — Alt text for the image

example.body: Master the fundamentals of EV technology, safety protocols, and maintenance best practices.

_graphic.alt: Modern electric vehicle at a charging station

components.description: Clean text block with heading and body. The workhorse component for explanatory content.
components.when_to_use: For introductory paragraphs, explanations, context-setting text, or any content that is primarily prose. Works well as the first block in a section to set context before interactive components.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for content that would benefit from visual structure (lists → checklist, steps → timeline, definitions → key-term).

required.body:
string (HTML) — Main content. Can include

,

    ,
      ,

      , , tags.

optional.displayTitle: string — Section heading above the body text
optional.instruction: string — Italic helper text (e.g. 'Read the following carefully')

example.body: Electric vehicles replace the internal combustion engine with an electric motor powered by a rechargeable battery pack. This fundamental difference affects every aspect of how the vehicle is maintained, repaired, and handled in emergencies.

components.description: Full-width image with hover zoom effect and optional caption. Provides visual breathing room between text-heavy sections.
components.when_to_use: For standalone images that deserve full attention — diagrams, photographs, infographics. Place between text sections to break up content rhythm.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for images paired with explanatory text (use graphic-text instead). Don't use for decorative images with no educational value.

_graphic.alt: Cutaway diagram showing battery cell arrangement in an EV chassis

example.body: A typical EV battery pack contains thousands of individual cells arranged in modules.

components.description: Side-by-side split layout with text on one side and image on the other. Alternates left/right for visual rhythm.
components.when_to_use: For content that pairs an explanation with a supporting image. Ideal for concept introductions, feature descriptions, or any text that benefits from a visual anchor. Alternate _imageAlign between sections for visual variety.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use back-to-back more than twice. If the image isn't directly related to the text, use separate text + graphic components.

required.displayTitle: string — Heading for the text side

optional._imageAlign: string — 'left' or 'right' (default: right). Alternate for visual rhythm.

example.body:
When you lift your foot off the accelerator, the electric motor reverses its role — becoming a generator that converts kinetic energy back into electrical energy stored in the battery.

This process can recover up to 70% of braking energy, extending range significantly.

_graphic.alt: Diagram showing energy flow during regenerative braking

components.description: Expandable panels with smooth animation and completion tracking. Each panel has a title and rich body content revealed on click.
components.when_to_use: For content with 3-8 distinct topics of roughly equal weight that learners can explore in any order. Great for: detailed explanations, safety procedures, FAQ-style content, or when you want to reduce initial visual overwhelm.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for sequential content (use narrative or timeline). Don't use with fewer than 3 items. Don't use for critical content that ALL learners must see (it's optional to expand).

required._items: array — 3-8 accordion panels, each with { title: string, body: string (HTML) }

optional.displayTitle: string — Section heading above the accordion
optional.body: string (HTML) — Intro text above the panels
optional.instruction: string — Helper text (e.g. 'Select each panel to learn more')

example.instruction: Select each panel to explore the safety features.

_items.title: High-Voltage Isolation
_items.body: EV battery packs operate at 400-800V. Multiple isolation barriers prevent current from reaching the vehicle chassis or occupants.

_items.title: Battery Management System (BMS)
_items.body: The BMS continuously monitors cell temperature, voltage, and current. It can disconnect the battery in milliseconds if anomalies are detected.

_items.title: Thermal Runaway Protection
_items.body: Modern EVs use liquid cooling, fire-resistant barriers between cells, and venting systems to prevent and contain thermal events.

components.description: Multiple-choice quiz with selection, submit, visual feedback, and retry. Supports single and multi-select modes.
components.when_to_use: For knowledge checks after a teaching section. Place after 3-5 content components to reinforce learning. Use the question to test comprehension, not recall of trivia.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't cluster multiple quizzes together. Space them throughout the course for better learning reinforcement.

required._items: array — Answer choices: [{ text: string, _shouldBeSelected: boolean }]

optional.instruction: string — Helper text (e.g. 'Choose the best answer')
optional._selectable: number — How many choices can be selected (default: 1). Use >1 for multi-select.

example.body: What is the typical voltage range of an EV battery pack?

_feedback.correct: Correct! Most modern EVs operate in the 400-800V range, with some performance vehicles reaching even higher.

_incorrect.final: Not quite. EV battery packs typically operate at 400-800V, which is why special safety protocols are essential.

components.description: Carousel slider with prev/next navigation, dot indicators, and optional images per slide. Content is presented sequentially.
components.when_to_use: For sequential content that tells a story or walks through a process step by step. Works well for: guided walkthroughs, case studies with multiple stages, or when showing progression. Use 3-7 slides.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for content that isn't sequential. Don't use for critical must-see content (users may skip slides). Don't use with fewer than 3 slides.

required._items: array — 3-7 slides: [{ title: string, body: string (HTML), _graphic?: { large: string, alt: string } }]

optional.displayTitle: string — Section heading above the slider
optional.body: string (HTML) — Intro text above the slider

_items.title: Step 1: Scene Assessment
_items.body: Before approaching the vehicle, assess the scene for immediate hazards: fire, fluid leaks, structural damage, or downed power lines.

_graphic.alt: First responder assessing an EV accident scene

_items.title: Step 2: Disable the Vehicle
_items.body: Locate and activate the high-voltage disconnect. This is typically found in the engine bay or under the rear seat.

_items.title: Step 3: Establish Safe Zone
_items.body: Create a 10-metre exclusion zone around the vehicle. Ensure all personnel wear appropriate PPE including insulated gloves rated to 1000V.

_graphic.alt: Exclusion zone diagram around damaged EV

components.description: Multi-card grid layout with optional image backgrounds. First card can span 2 columns for visual hierarchy. Cards have hover effects.
components.when_to_use: For presenting 3-6 related concepts, features, or topics at a glance. Perfect for: feature overviews, key benefits, topic summaries, or any content where items are peers (not sequential).
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for sequential content (use narrative or timeline). Don't use with fewer than 3 items. Don't use for long-form content that needs full reading.

required._items: array — 3-6 cards: [{ title: string, body: string (HTML), _graphic?: { large: string, src: string, alt: string } }]

optional.displayTitle: string — Section heading above the grid
optional.body: string (HTML) — Intro text above the grid

_items.title: Level 1 — Home Outlet
_items.body: Standard 120V outlet. Adds 5-8km of range per hour. Best for overnight charging at home.

_items.title: Level 2 — Dedicated Charger
_items.body: 240V installation. Adds 40-50km of range per hour. Ideal for home and workplace charging.

_items.title: DC Fast Charging
_items.body: Direct current at 50-350kW. Can charge to 80% in 20-40 minutes. Found at public charging stations.

components.description: Auto-formatted data table with sticky header, alternating rows, and monospace number styling. Parses structured data from HTML.
components.when_to_use: For comparative data, specifications, reference tables, or any structured information with consistent columns. Great for: specs comparison, schedules, reference data.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for simple two-column comparisons (use comparison instead). Don't use for narrative content forced into table format.

required.body:
string (HTML) — Table data as
    /
  • items or

    lines, with columns separated by ' - ', ' : ', or ' | '

optional.displayTitle: string — Table title (shown as sticky header)

example.body:
• Battery Capacity | 75 kWh
  • Voltage | 400V
  • Weight | 450 kg
  • Warranty | 8 years / 160,000 km
  • Cycle Life | 1,500+ cycles

components.description: Video player with custom play/pause overlay, poster image, and hover controls bar.
components.when_to_use: For video content extracted from the SCORM package. Only use when actual video files exist in the media inventory.
components.when_not_to_use: Never fabricate video sources. Only reference videos that exist in the content bucket's media inventory.

example.body: Watch how the battery management system responds to a simulated fault condition.

components.description: Multi-field form with labeled text inputs and submit button. Used for reflection prompts or data entry activities.
components.when_to_use: For reflective exercises where learners type their own responses. Great for: personal action plans, reflection questions, note-taking activities.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for assessed content (use mcq). Don't use for simple yes/no questions.

required._items: array — Form fields: [{ prefix: string (label), placeholder?: string }]

example.body: Based on what you've learned, document your key takeaways and next steps.

_items.placeholder: What stood out most to you?

_items.prefix: How will you apply this in your role?

_items.placeholder: What would you like to explore further?

components.description: Selectable option cards with letter labels (A, B, C...) and selection indicator. For scenario-based choices or self-assessment.
components.when_to_use: For scenario-based learning where learners choose a path or option. Good for: decision-making exercises, self-assessment, preference selection.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for graded questions (use mcq). Don't use with more than 5 options.

required.displayTitle: string — The scenario or question heading
required.body:
string (HTML) — Options as
  • items or

    paragraphs (parsed into cards)

example.body:
• Immediately approach the vehicle to check for injuries
  • Call emergency services and establish a safe perimeter
  • Attempt to disconnect the battery yourself
  • Wait for the vehicle manufacturer's emergency response team

components.description: Numbered steps connected by a vertical line. Each step has a number badge, title, and body content. Scroll-triggered reveal animation.
components.when_to_use: For sequential processes, historical timelines, or step-by-step procedures where ORDER matters. Use 3-8 steps. Each step should be a distinct phase or milestone.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for non-sequential content (use accordion or bento). Don't use for very short steps with just a title (use checklist).

required._items: array — 3-8 steps: [{ title: string, body: string (HTML) }]

optional.body: string (HTML) — Intro text above the timeline

_items.title: Monthly Check
_items.body: Inspect tyre pressure, washer fluid, and charge cable condition. Check for any dashboard warning lights.

_items.title: Every 6 Months
_items.body: Rotate tyres, inspect brake pads (they last longer on EVs due to regenerative braking), and check coolant levels.

_items.title: Annual Service
_items.body: Full diagnostic scan, cabin air filter replacement, battery health check, and software updates.

_items.title: Every 2 Years
_items.body: Replace brake fluid, inspect suspension components, and check high-voltage cable insulation integrity.

components.description: Side-by-side comparison columns with check/cross icons for features. Clean, scannable format for comparing two or three options.
components.when_to_use: For comparing 2-3 options, plans, approaches, or technologies across multiple criteria. Great for: pros/cons, feature comparisons, before/after.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for data-heavy tables (use data-table). Don't compare more than 3 columns.

required.columns: array — 2-3 column definitions: [{ title: string, subtitle?: string }]
required.rows: array — Comparison rows: [{ label: string, values: array of (true | false | string) per column }]

components.description: Large prominent numbers or statistics with labels and optional context. Animated count-up on scroll. Displayed in a row of 2-4 stats.
components.when_to_use: For impactful statistics, key metrics, or numbers that deserve emphasis. Place after introductory content to reinforce with data. Use 2-4 stats that tell a compelling story together.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for non-numeric content. Don't use more than 4 stats (gets overwhelming).

required._items: array — 2-4 stats: [{ value: string, label: string, suffix?: string, prefix?: string }]

_items.label: Reduction in moving parts vs ICE

components.description: Emphasized text with a vertical accent bar and optional attribution. Draws attention to key messages or expert quotes.
components.when_to_use: For highlighting a critical insight, expert quote, or key message that learners should remember. Use sparingly — 1-2 per course maximum. Place after the content it summarizes.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't overuse. Don't use for generic text that doesn't deserve emphasis.

required.body: string — The quote or key message text (plain text, not HTML)

example.body: The shift to electric vehicles isn't just about changing fuel sources — it's about fundamentally rethinking how we approach vehicle safety and maintenance.

components.description: Highlighted vocabulary term with its definition. Styled with a distinctive background and icon to stand out from body text.
components.when_to_use: For introducing domain-specific terminology that learners need to know. Place immediately before or after the content that uses the term. Can group 2-5 terms together.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for common words. Don't use for full paragraphs of explanation (use text instead).

required._items: array — 1-5 terms: [{ term: string, definition: string }]

_items.definition: An uncontrolled chain reaction in battery cells where rising temperature causes further heat generation, potentially leading to fire or explosion.
The current battery charge level expressed as a percentage of total capacity, similar to a fuel gauge.
A system that recovers kinetic energy during deceleration by using the electric motor as a generator to recharge the battery.

components.description: Checkable items with completion tracking and a progress indicator. Items can be ticked off by the learner.
components.when_to_use: For action lists, pre-task checklists, safety procedures, or any content where learners should confirm they've understood each item. Good for: PPE requirements, pre-work checklists, compliance items.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for informational lists without actionable items (use text with <ul>). Don't use for sequential steps (use timeline).

required._items: array — 3-10 items: [{ text: string, detail?: string }]

optional.instruction: string — Helper text (e.g. 'Check each item as you complete it')

example.instruction: Confirm each item before beginning work on an EV.

_items.text: Insulated gloves rated to 1000V are available and inspected
_items.detail: Check for tears, holes, or signs of damage before each use.

_items.text: Vehicle is powered off and key is removed
_items.detail: Verify 'Ready' light on dashboard is off.

_items.text: High-voltage service disconnect is confirmed open
Area is clear of water and conductive materials
Emergency response plan is reviewed and understood

components.description: Horizontal tabbed interface where each tab reveals different content. Only one tab visible at a time.
components.when_to_use: For 2-6 categories of content that are alternatives (not sequential). Users choose which to view. Great for: role-based content, topic categories, different perspectives on the same subject.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for sequential content (use narrative). Don't use with more than 6 tabs (use accordion). Don't nest tabs inside tabs.

required._items: array — 2-6 tabs: [{ title: string, body: string (HTML), _graphic?: { large: string, alt: string } }]

optional.displayTitle: string — Section heading above the tabs

_items.title: Battery Pack
_items.body: The battery pack is the most expensive component, typically comprising 30-40% of the vehicle's total cost. It consists of modules containing thousands of individual lithium-ion cells.

_items.title: Electric Motor
_items.body: Modern EVs use permanent magnet or induction motors that convert electrical energy to mechanical motion. They deliver instant torque from zero RPM.

_items.title: Power Electronics
_items.body: The inverter converts DC battery power to AC for the motor. The onboard charger handles AC-to-DC conversion during charging.

components.description: Flip-card interaction showing a term or question on the front and the answer/definition on the back. Click to flip with 3D rotation animation.
components.when_to_use: For vocabulary review, Q&A pairs, or any content where learners should try to recall before seeing the answer. Good for: term/definition drilling, review activities, memory reinforcement.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for first-time content delivery (learners haven't learned the terms yet). Don't use for complex multi-paragraph content. Place after the teaching section.

required._items: array — 3-8 cards: [{ front: string, back: string }]

optional.instruction: string — Helper text (e.g. 'Click each card to reveal the answer')

example.instruction: Click each card to test your knowledge.

_items.front: What voltage range do EV batteries typically operate at?
_items.back: 400-800V DC, with some performance vehicles exceeding 800V.
A system that uses the electric motor as a generator during deceleration to convert kinetic energy back into stored electrical energy.

_items.front: What PPE is required for EV high-voltage work?
_items.back: Class 0 insulated gloves (rated to 1000V), safety glasses, and insulated tools.

components.description: An image with numbered hotspot markers at specific positions. Clicking a marker reveals a tooltip or sidebar with information about that point.
components.when_to_use: For diagrams, equipment photos, or illustrations where specific parts need identification. Great for: anatomy diagrams, equipment labels, spatial relationships.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use if the image is simple enough for a caption (use graphic). Don't fabricate hotspot positions — this component requires the AI to generate an appropriate image.

required._markers: array — 3-8 hotspots: [{ x: number (0-100 %), y: number (0-100 %), label: string, body: string }]

optional.instruction: string — Helper text (e.g. 'Click each marker to learn more')

example.instruction: Click each numbered marker to explore the components.

_graphic.alt: Cutaway view of an EV battery pack showing internal components

_markers.label: Battery Cells
_markers.body: Individual lithium-ion cells arranged in series and parallel to achieve target voltage and capacity.

_markers.label: Cooling System
_markers.body: Liquid cooling channels that maintain optimal cell temperature between 20-40°C.

_markers.label: BMS Module
_markers.body: The Battery Management System that monitors and balances all cells.

_markers.label: HV Connector
_markers.body: High-voltage connector with safety interlock that disconnects during service.

components.description: Connected workflow nodes showing a process from start to end. Each node has a title and description, connected by directional arrows.
components.when_to_use: For workflows, decision trees, or any process where the flow and connections matter. Great for: troubleshooting flows, business processes, system architecture overviews.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use for simple linear steps (use timeline). Best for processes with 3-6 nodes.

required._nodes: array — 3-6 process nodes: [{ title: string, body: string, type?: 'start' | 'process' | 'decision' | 'end' }]

_nodes.title: Check Battery Level
_nodes.body: Read the current State of Charge on the dashboard.

_nodes.title: Below 20%?
_nodes.body: If SoC is below 20%, proceed to charge immediately.

_nodes.title: Select Charger Type
_nodes.body: Choose between Level 2 (if time available) or DC Fast Charge (if urgent).

_nodes.title: Connect & Charge
_nodes.body: Plug in, authenticate, and monitor charging progress via the app.

_nodes.title: Charged to 80%+
_nodes.body: Disconnect once target SoC is reached. Avoid routinely charging to 100%.

components.description: Grid or masonry layout of multiple images with lightbox zoom on click. Captions shown on hover.
components.when_to_use: For showcasing multiple related images — equipment photos, examples, visual references. Use when images from the SCORM package deserve to be shown together. 3-8 images.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't fabricate many images just to fill a gallery. Only use when the content genuinely benefits from showing multiple visuals.

required._items: array — 3-8 images: [{ _graphic: { large: string, alt: string }, caption?: string }]

_items.caption: Type 1 / J1772 — Standard in North America
CHAdeMO — Japanese standard DC fast charging

components.description: Edge-to-edge full-width image with optional text overlay. Creates visual impact and section breaks. Parallax scroll effect.
components.when_to_use: For dramatic visual section dividers between major topics. Use to create breathing room and visual impact. 1-3 per course maximum.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't overuse. Don't use for images that need captions or detailed study (use graphic or labeled-image).

example.body: As EV adoption accelerates, your expertise in safety and maintenance becomes increasingly vital.

_graphic.alt: Fleet of electric vehicles on a modern highway at sunset

components.description: Video player with an expandable transcript panel below. Combines media playback with accessible text content.
components.when_to_use: Only when both a video file AND its transcript text are available in the content bucket. Enhances accessibility and allows learners to read along or search the content.
components.when_not_to_use: Don't use if there's no video (use text). Don't use if there's no transcript (use media).

example.transcript:
Interviewer: Can you explain the safety systems built into modern EV batteries?

Dr. Chen: Absolutely. Modern battery packs have multiple layers of protection...

design_rules.visual_variety: Never place two consecutive components of the same type. Alternate between content-heavy and visual components.
design_rules.section_rhythm: Each section should follow a pattern: context (text/graphic-text) → detail (accordion/tabs/narrative) → reinforce (mcq/flashcard/checklist). Not every section needs all three.
design_rules.image_alternation: When using multiple graphic-text components, alternate _imageAlign between 'left' and 'right'.
design_rules.assessment_spacing: Space MCQ components at least 3-5 components apart. Place after teaching, not before.
design_rules.hero_rule: Every course starts with exactly one hero component.
design_rules.max_components_per_section: Keep sections to 3-6 components. If a section grows beyond 6, split it into two sections.
design_rules.visual_breaks: Use full-bleed, stat-callout, or pullquote between dense content sections to create breathing room.
